entry.title: Welcome to Season 10 of SAP Let’s Talk Data!
entry.content:
Season 10 of SAP Let’s Talk Data is built for practitioners who make data work. We’re shifting from high-level storytelling to hard-earned, technical lessons from engineers, scientists, stewards, and architects who design, operate, and scale real-world data systems.

This season we’re putting the spotlight on the people who make data work: the data professionals shaping how businesses run. From data engineers and scientists to stewards, analysts, and architects, these roles don’t operate in isolation. Together they balance trust, governance, scale, and speed to turn data into real business impact.

Season 10 is about how ideas become impact. Expect candid conversations with practitioners who lead data operations, build analytics products, steward sensitive information, and translate insights into action. We’ll highlight real challenges—conflicting priorities, organizational friction, and technical trade-offs—and lessons learned from teams that turned those challenges into advantages. Whether you’re building data models, owning data quality, or shaping strategy, these episodes are designed to give you usable perspectives, actionable tips, and a clearer view of how data professionals connect to the broader business mission.

Because data professionals aren’t just supporting the business, they’re driving it forward.

Here is a look at the first episodes

  • A Day in the Life of a Data Steward — Behind the Scenes of Trusted Data

What does a day in the life of a data steward really look like? This podcast pulls back the curtain on the people who keep enterprise data trustworthy, usable, and compliant. We will discuss how these data steward navigate through the rhythms of their day—triaging data incidents, curating metadata, negotiating business definitions, enforcing access controls, and coordinating fixes across engineering, analytics, and business teams. We explore both process realities and the best practices in place to ensure trust.

  • All in on AI: SAP BDC and SAP Business AI - coming soon

Leona Hasani is an SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) expert who specializes in all things data analysis and data management for BDC. In this podcast, she will dive deep into how BDC can help a data scientist leverage all the BDC features and technologies to maximize the value. If you are a data scientist, work with one, or in general, manage some form of the data environment in your team, then this podcast will help you understand all the tools available to you when using SAP Business Data Cloud.

Join us!

Subscribe to follow the season and join the conversation as we lift the curtain on the people and practices powering data-driven organizations.

  • Spotify
  • Apple Podcasts
  • YouTube
  • And more…

What further connection for collaboration and growth?

Join the conversation with the Data Professionals Community!

The group welcomes perspectives across all data and AI topics. These types of projects deliver their greatest value when a team of colleagues work together towards the final solution. Thus, the spirit of this community.

entry.title: NEW SAP Master Data Governance MDG100 E-Learning Course !
entry.content:
Unlock the Power of Data with the New SAP Master Data Governance E-Learning Course

In today’s fast-paced business environment, effective data management is the cornerstone of success. SAP is proud to introduce its latest MDG100 e-learning course, SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA, designed to empower professionals like Data Stewards with the skills and knowledge to master data governance and drive business excellence.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-master-data-governance-on-sap-s-4hana

Why Choose SAP Master Data Governance?

SAP Master Data Governance is a critical solution for organizations aiming to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and compliance across their enterprise. This course provides a comprehensive understanding of SAP’s MDG solution strategy, covering essential data domains, processes, and business applications.

What You’ll Learn

This expertly crafted course is structured into 9 units, each tailored to build your expertise step by step:

  • Unit 1: Introducing SAP Master Data Governance
    Gain foundational knowledge of MDG with 4 lessons in just 48 minutes.
  • Unit 2: Distinguishing Master Data Governance for Material
    Dive deeper into material-specific governance with 2 lessons spanning 1 hr and 23 minutes.
  • Unit 3: Describing Master Data Governance for Business Partner, Supplier, Customer
    Explore the intricacies of managing business partner data in 3 lessons over 2 hrs and 33 minutes.
  • Unit 4: Introducing Master Data Governance for Financials and Hierarchy Management
    Understand financial data governance in 1 lesson lasting 1 hr and 8 minutes.
  • Unit 5: Describing Master Data Governance - Mass Changes and Multiple Object Processing
    Learn about mass changes and object processing in 3 lessons totaling 1 hr and 5 minutes.
  • Unit 6: Discussing Master Data Governance - Mass Processing and Consolidation
    Master the art of mass processing and consolidation in 3 lessons over 1 hr and 13 minutes.
  • Unit 7: Describing Process Analytics in SAP Master Data Governance
    Discover how to leverage process analytics in MDG with 2 lessons in 44 minutes.
  • Unit 8: Introducing Master Data Governance - Data Quality Management
    Ensure data quality with 4 lessons in 57 minutes.
  • Unit 9: Describing Master Data Governance - Cloud Ready Mode
    Explore cloud-ready MDG capabilities in 5 lessons lasting 24 minutes.

Learning Outcomes

By completing this course, you will be able to:

  • Present SAP’s MDG solution strategy with confidence.
  • Explain the data domains and processes integral to MDG.
  • Understand and apply business processes within MDG to optimize data governance.

Your Learning Journey

Whether you’re just starting out or looking to deepen your expertise, this course is designed to meet your needs:

  • Building the Basics: Lay the foundation for understanding SAP MDG.
  • Develop Your Expertise: Gain practical insights into MDG processes and applications.
  • Skill Deepening Learning: Enhance your knowledge with advanced concepts.
  • Excel in Your Expertise: Achieve mastery in SAP MDG.
  • Advanced Specialization Learning: Specialize in specific areas of MDG for targeted expertise.

Why Enroll?

This e-learning course is your gateway to becoming a master in data governance, enabling you to drive efficiency, ensure compliance, and unlock the full potential of SAP S/4HANA. Whether you’re an aspiring data professional or a seasoned expert, this course will equip you with the tools to excel in your role.

Start Learning Today!

  • Don’t miss the opportunity to elevate your career and make a strategic move towards mastering data governance. 
  • Enroll in the SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA e-learning course today and take the first step toward becoming a data governance expert. 
  • Empower your organization with the knowledge to transform data into actionable insights.

    The future of data governance starts with you!

entry.title: SAP MDG + Azure AD - Part 1: The Why, The Gap, and What Becomes Possible
entry.content:
Hi Peers, I'm Prem, an SAP MDG Techno-Functional Consultant. This is my first blog on SCN, and I wanted to share something genuine gap and a to be use case in MDG. I didn't want to rewrite SAP documentation. Instead, I aimed to provide a real insight into something I got my hands on, figured out, ran into issues and ultimately got working.

In one of the client projects I'm working on, we needed to sync user information from the client's Azure Active Directory with SAP. This meant keeping identity data up to date across both systems. The business wanted user profiles, contact details, and organizational data from Azure AD to be accessible in the MDG landscape without having data stewards enter it manually each time. The question was clear: how do we get SAP to read from Azure Active Directory? This led me to the ABAP SDK for Azure - an open-source toolkit supported by Microsoft. What began as a simple exploration turned into a full end-to-end integration. We set up SAP to authenticate with Azure AD using OAuth2, call the Microsoft Graph API, and pull live user data into the system.

The setup had its share of trial and errors, a few configuration missteps, some back and forth with IDs and certificates. What helped me get through it was the ABAP SDK for Azure repository on GitHub and the work its contributors have put into the documentation and demo programs. Solid resource - I'd recommend bookmarking it before you start

However after all these hiccups it works now. Throughout this process, I gained a strong Introductory Understanding of OAuth2, SSL certificate management, cloud identity concepts, and, most importantly, what Azure Active Directory is and how it fits into an SAP MDG landscape.

Now, what is Azure Active Directory?

Azure Active Directory recently renamed Microsoft Entra ID is where most companies manage their employee identities. Each person in the organization has a profile there, including their name, email, department, job title, manager, and the applications they can access. It serves as the single source of truth for who works in the organization and what they are allowed to do. If SAP MDG is where your organization manages critical business master data, Azure AD is where it manages people data. Connecting the two ensures that your master data in SAP stays current and accurate, just like your HR directory.

The Real Gap

Azure AD has the people data, while SAP MDG has the business data. Both are within the same organization, but they do not communicate. Data stewards manually bridge this gap, which is not a data quality issue. It is an integration gap.

 

 

 

What becomes possible once SAP can communicate with Azure AD?

Here’s what opens up in MDG with this connection:

  • User onboarding: When a new employee is created in Azure AD, SAP reads that profile and automatically provisions their MDG roles and workflow assignments. No tickets or waiting involved.
  • Business Partner enrichment: Information like job title, department, phone number, and reporting manager resides in Azure AD. Instead of manual typing by data stewards, the system pulls this information directly. It becomes accurate and requires no manual input.
  • Contact validation: If a data steward enters a contact person on a vendor record, the system validates it against Azure AD in real time. It checks whether this person exists in the organization. If not, it raises a flag before the change request can proceed.
  • Dynamic workflow routing: Rather than having fixed approvers, the workflow reads the requestor's manager directly from Azure AD. If the approver changes roles, the routing automatically adjusts.
  • Access governance: Periodically comparing MDG role assignments with Azure AD helps identify if someone has left the organization but still holds active MDG roles. These roles can then be flagged for removal. No more inactive users remaining in governance roles.

Five use cases, one integration, one endpoint.

 

What we built and how it works

The technical setup involves SAP authenticating with Azure AD, receiving an OAuth2 token, and using that token to call the Microsoft Graph API. It requires one RFC destination for authentication, another for the API, three configuration tables, and the ABAP SDK for Azure manages the rest.

I will guide you through every step including every mistake I have gone through in the upcoming parts of this series.

What’s next?

This was the why part & Part 2 will cover the how. You'll see a complete technical walkthrough, from creating an Azure account to seeing user data appear in your SAP system

 Stay tuned.

entry.title: 切换到 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中全新的业务伙伴主数据批量处理的应用
entry.content:
前言

你是否仍在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中使用已被弃用的业务伙伴（Business Partner，简称 BP）主数据批量导入和批量维护应用？

现在正是时候切换到新的继任应用（Successor Apps），以便在旧应用被正式移除之前完成系统准备。本篇文章将为你介绍：

  • 为什么需要进行切换
  • 新应用带来的主要改进
  • 切换所需的具体步骤和注意事项

⚠️ 重要提醒
在旧应用被彻底下线之前，你必须完成本文描述的切换操作。

当前计划：旧应用将在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608 版本中移除。

为什么要切换到新的批处理应用？

新的业务伙伴主数据批处理应用在多个方面得到了显著增强：

1. 更好的易用性（Usability）

  • 启动批量变更流程时，提供了全新的对话框
  • 可以更加灵活地定义和调整选择条件
  • 选择条件可在页面顶部直接查看和进一步细化
  • 不再需要单独定义变更“范围（Scope）”

➡️ 整体操作更加直观、符合业务用户的使用习惯。

 

 

2. 批量变更与批量导入更加集中

  • 现在可以在同一个起始对话框中：
    • 直接基于文件执行批量变更
    • 或进行批量数据导入
  • “管理批量维护（Manage Mass Maintenance）” 应用成为统一入口
  • 不再需要单独的 “Start Mass Processing” 磁贴

3. 更丰富的流程属性

  • 新版本支持更多流程属性，例如：
    • 到期日期（Due Date）
    • 其他流程相关信息

4. 与 SAP MDG 的更深度集成

  • 对于已购买 SAP Master Data Governance（MDG） 许可证的客户：
    • 可在 “Manage Business Partner – Central Governance” 中查看单个业务伙伴
    • 实现业务伙伴的统一视图
  • 可通过后文提到的 Additional Scope Items 启用此能力

5. 最新的 SAP Fiori 用户体验

  • 使用最新的 SAP Fiori 设计语言
  • 支持通用 Fiori 功能，例如：
    • 与其他用户协作
    • 更一致的交互体验

我需要做什么？（切换步骤概览）

完成切换总共需要 三个基础步骤：

步骤一：完成或清理已有批量流程

  • 请先：
    • 完成所有仍在进行中的批量维护流程
    • 或删除不再需要的流程

📌 原因说明
使用旧版 “Manage Mass Maintenance” 创建的流程，在新版本应用中将不可见。

步骤二：激活新功能并调整业务目录

1️⃣ 激活功能开关

激活功能：

Successor MDG apps for business partners
(MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY)

⚠️ 注意：该功能激活后不可回退，请先在测试环境验证相关业务流程。

请打开系统配置里的Activate Features来激活此功能：

 

2️⃣ 移除已弃用的业务目录

从业务角色中移除以下目录（Deprecated）：

  • Mass Load - Business Partner Master
    SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSLOAD_PC
  • Mass Maintenance - Business Partner Master
    SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSMAINT_PC

3️⃣ 分配新的继任业务目录

添加新的业务目录：

  • Master Data Governance - Mass Maintenance for Business Partners
    SAP_CMD_BC_BPCS_MAINT_PC

这将使你的业务角色与最新版模板保持一致：

  • Master Data Specialist - Business Partner Data
    SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_SPECIALIST

针对2️⃣3️⃣两步的操作，你可以在维护业务角色中，找到你做批量维护的业务角色，手动删除废弃的业务目录，和添加新的业务目录：

 或者

你也可以在应用“在升级后管理业务角色更改”中，打开“不赞成使用的业务目录”页签，选择被废弃的业务目录

 然后对受影响的业务角色直接做更改。这样操作会直接把被废弃的业务目录替换成后继新的业务目录：

 

步骤三：通过导出完成技术设置（关键步骤）

这是一个强制性步骤，用于最终启用所有新应用。

操作步骤：

  1. 启动应用
    Export Master Data – Business Partners
    （Fiori ID：F6551A）

  2. 定义一个最小范围的导出，例如：

    • 表：BUT000
    • 至少选择 一个业务伙伴

  3. 执行导出

    • 不需要下载生成的文件
    • 只要导出成功完成即可

✅ 完成后，即可正式使用新应用：

  • Manage Mass Maintenance – BP（Fiori ID：F7974）
  • 以及新业务目录中包含的所有继任应用

其他扩展范围（Additional Scope Items）

如果你拥有 SAP Master Data Governance 的额外许可证，还可以启用以下 Scope Item：

  • Central Governance for Business Partner (1N7)

这将为业务伙伴主数据治理提供更多高级能力。

总结

通过切换到新的业务伙伴主数据批处理应用，你将获得：

  • 更现代化的 SAP Fiori 体验
  • 更灵活、更直观的批量处理方式
  • 与 SAP MDG 更深度的集成
  • 为未来版本升级（2608 及以后）提前做好准备

👉 建议尽早完成切换，并在生产系统启用前充分测试。

原文出处

SAP Community Blog：
Switch to the new mass apps for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: The Challenges You Face and How SAP Business Data Cloud Helps
entry.content:
Data stewards are accountable for data quality, compliance, and trust, but operate across fragmented systems, manual processes, and growing regulatory pressure—often without clear ownership or visibility.  For data stewards, SAP Business Data Cloud is not “more governance tooling.” It is a way to operationalize governance, scale trust, and turn stewardship into a measurable business enabler.

This is where SAP Business Data Cloud changes the conversation. It gives data stewards a governed, business‑ready foundation where business context, semantics, data, and usage all come together. Governance isn’t separate from analytics—it’s embedded in how data is shared and consumed.

Let’s talk a bit about problems and solutions:\

Fragmented data and inconsistent definitions

Your challenge:
Key business data is spread across SAP and non‑SAP systems, often defined differently by different teams. This makes it hard to enforce standards or build confidence in reporting and analytics.

How SAP Business Data Cloud helps:

  • Brings business data together with a consistent semantic layer
  • Helps align technical data with shared business definitions
  • Makes governed, trusted data easier to discover and use across analytics and AI scenarios

What this means for you:
Less time reconciling definitions. More confidence that people are working from the same understanding of the data.

Manual governance and limited visibility

Your challenge:
Governance work often lives in documents, spreadsheets, or disconnected tools, making it hard to track ownership, issues, and progress.

How SAP Business Data Cloud helps:

  • Connects governance concepts directly to live data assets and processes
  • Improves visibility into where data comes from, how it’s used, and who is responsible
  • Supports more consistent, repeatable governance practices instead of one‑off fixes

What this means for you:
You can focus on managing and improving data, not chasing information or enforcing rules manually.

Compliance pressure without operational support

Your challenge:
You’re expected to support regulatory and internal compliance, but controls are often indirect or hard to demonstrate during audits.

How SAP Business Data Cloud helps:

  • Enables clearer lineage, transparency, and traceability across data flows
  • Helps connect governance policies to actual data usage
  • Makes it easier to show how data is managed, protected, and governed in practice

What this means for you:
Stronger audit readiness and reduced risk—without slowing down the business.

Building trust with business and IT stakeholders

Your challenge:
Business users want speed. IT teams want stability. You need both to trust the data and the governance behind it.

How SAP Business Data Cloud helps:

  • Provides a shared foundation where business and technical teams work from the same governed data
  • Supports analytics and AI use cases without bypassing governance
  • Helps you communicate the value of governance in terms business leaders understand

What this means for you:
You gain credibility as a trusted partner, not a blocker—strengthening your influence in the buying and decision‑making process.

Again, it’s not about “more governance.” It’s about giving data stewards the structure, transparency, and leverage they need to protect and elevate the value of enterprise data.

Want to learn more about SAP Business Data Cloud as it applies to your role?  Check out this guide: One Foundation. Every Role. Smarter Insights.

entry.title: Deactivation of finance master data objects in S/4 HANA cloud private edition - Cost centers
entry.content:
Hi All,

Finance master data is a well governed metadata object as its driven by financial reporting requirements. GL chart of accounts are defined as per the external reporting requirements of US-GAPP, IFRS etc. Cost centers are used to drive internal management reporting to understand the expenses and costs incurred by various products/functions while profit centers drive management reporting of overall profitability of  products and services. Profit centers also drive the revenue /cost of revenue numbers for reporting.

Over a period of time organizations business models evolve and certain teams, services are wind down, merged etc. However most of the times the associated metadata elements are still active and lying in your ERP landscape dormant. Over a period of  ~5 years the organization would have gone through multiple changes and you will have lot of cost centers, profit centers, WBS & internal order which are active but never used. 

IMPORTANCE OF DEACTIVATION:

Business perspective:

1. It avoids accidental booking of invoices, expense reports or even manual journals to obsolete cost objects.

2. If a planner has 20 cost centers under him but only 5 are being actively used, he might have to manually put 0 or NA comments for rest 15 cost centers in every forecast cycle.

3. It safe guards allocation runs from allocating costs to outdated organizational unit.

Systems Perspective:

1. Every time a financial analyst/Accounting controller pulls a report, the runtime of the these reports are faster as its querying a smaller subset of data.

2.Reconciliation of master data across different systems finance, CRM, procurement , HR is easier and any discrepancy can be identified efficiently.

3. It helps to maintain a better data quality score as we need to focus only on actively used objects rather than all active objects in the system.

Now lets talk about how we achieve the deactivation of cost center which was previously being used to track Opex ( both Payroll as well as non payroll ) 

CRITERIA FOR DEACTIVATION FOR COST CENTERS

The deactivation trigger can come from :

1. Deactivation due to changes in organizational structure.

2. Dormant cost centers for a certain period of time defined by the finance team of an organization.

PRE-REQUISTIES FOR DEACTIVATION

1. Any cost center should not have any recurring activity on BS or P&L accounts. Use the GL line items display app to check for activity. This should be done for all ledgers.

2. Fixed Assets: Check for fixed assets which are tagged to the cost center. If there are any active assets which have NBV value and a depreciation schedule then asset master has to be adjusted and a balance reclass of the NBV needs to be performed.

3. Accruals:  There might be prepaids tagged to a cost center, e.g.; payment for service done at once but accrued over 12 months, leases etc. In such cases if the prepaids are still being amortized then adjust the account assignment  ( cost center ) in the accrual engine and also reclass the remaining value on the balance sheet to the new cost center.

4. Headcount/Payroll: If a particular team or department was wound down, then the existing headcounts needs to be transferred from the existing cost center to new cost center. In some cases if a cost center has 0 headcount doesn't mean that payroll activity will cease to exist. There might be cases where there are pending liabilities which the company has to recover from employee who has left from the cost center or the company has net payables to employees or some pending taxation. It's best to align with payroll teams to take their confirmation.

5. Purchase orders/Service entry sheets:  All purchase orders needs to be closed. This will ensure there are no invoices raised against a PO once the CC is closed.  Similarly all the service entry sheets either have to be closed or has to be updated with new account assignment to avoid errors.

6. WBS elements -> Any WBS element which is settling to cost center needs to be also reviewed and updated to ensure the settlement runs don't result in any errors.

7. Balance sheet balances: Any balance sheet balance has to be cleared/reclassed to alternate cost centers with same account assignments in all currencies ( company code currency, transaction currency , group currency ) . This is to ensure that there is no impact to the revaluation and currency translation processes during month ends.

8 Budgeting/Forecasting : Before a cost center is closed, ensure the budget is moved to central cost pool or to a alternate cost center to ensure that budget is not blocked and can be utilized. The forecasting should also be updated to reflect the forecast values in new cost centers.

What MDG has done in S/4 HANA 2023 & beyond?

MDG now has provided the capability to perform remote checks for cost center from a hub system to your central transactional S/4 system to ensure that before you submit a blocking request you know that it doesn't have any lingering balances. More details can be found in note 

3517066 - MDG-F: Validation of changes in (remote or local) S/4 HANA systems

However this doesn't cover all the pre-requisites which i have mentioned above.

Operational challenges: 

 In any large organization, the processes across finance, supply chain, HR, CRM are running on various platforms both on-prem and cloud. To give a simple example SAP S/4 HANA will be the single source of truth for all financials, however the upstream processes like CRM could be running on SaaS platform, HR processes might be running on some non-SAP 3rd party tool, similarly planning and budgeting could be running on some in-house developed tool. With so many systems even though SAP MDG is used maintaining the data lineage is a challenge  as there will be a multistep integrations. Also there might be some systems which are not at all integrated to operational issues and technical challenges.

CONCLUSION

Proactive deactivation of dormant cost objects is essential for maintaining a lean, high-performing financial ecosystem that prevents reporting errors and operational friction. While modern tools like MDG-F provide a foundation, a truly robust data lifecycle requires seamless cross-functional alignment to ensure long-term integrity across complex, multi-platform landscapes.

*This is my own experience of working on this topic, and not a comprehensive list of all the checks one needs to perform. However this might be a good starting point if you are looking to deactivate cost centers.*

SAP Master Data Governance  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Finance 

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: SAP MDG + Azure AD - Part 2: Connecting SAP to Azure Active Directory – The Technical Setup
entry.content:
Hi Peers, 

In Part 1, I covered the business case why SAP MDG needs Azure Active Directory and the five use cases it enables. If you haven't read that, I'd recommend starting there for context. Below is the link for Part 1.

SAP MDG + Azure AD - Part 1: The Why, The Gap, and... - SAP Community

In this post, I'll walk you through the complete setup Azure account, app registration, client secrets, API permissions, RFC destinations, SSL certificates, configuration tables, and finally running the program that fetches Azure AD user data into SAP.

Here's the flow in technical terms. SAP sends an HTTP POST to Azure AD's token endpoint with the Client ID and Client Secret. Azure validates these credentials and returns a JWT bearer token. SAP then sends an HTTP GET to the Microsoft Graph API /users endpoint with that token in the Authorization header. Graph API validates the token, checks the app's permissions, and returns user data as JSON. SAP deserializes the JSON into an ABAP internal table.

Two HTTP calls. Two RFC destinations. Three config tables. That's the architecture.

Before jumping in What helped me get through this was the ABAP SDK for Azure repository on GitHub and the work its contributors have put into the documentation and demo programs. Solid resource , I’d recommend you to have a look into it if you need any further clarification.

Now Lets split this technical Deep dive into two parts based on Azure configuration and SAP configuration

Part A: Azure Side Configuration

Step 1: Set Up Your Azure Account and Tenant

Sign up for Azure and follow the below steps to set up a new Tenant, Once logged in, go to All Services → Microsoft Entra ID → Manage Tenant → Create a new Tenant. Note down the Tenant ID you will need this later when configuring the RFC destination in SAP.

Step 2: Register an Application

Now let us Register and Application, this application you register here represents your SAP system in Azure. It gives SAP an identity to authenticate with Azure services.

For this Go to App Registrations → New Registration → Enter a name for your application (e.g., ABAPSDK-DEMO-APP) → Select "Single tenant only" → Click Register.

Step 3: Note the Key Values

After registration, the Overview page displays two values you need to save:

Application ID : Unique ID used to identify our application in ABAP Program

Directory (Tenant ID): This Unique ID represents our organization and this will be used in SM59 as a prefix for the path to identify our Tenant with SAP

Copy both and keep them safe. You will use them throughout the SAP configuration.

Step 4: Generate a Client Secret

Under the registered application, go to Certificates & Secrets → Create a new Client Secret → Set an expiration date → Click Add.

Azure will display two columns: Value and Secret ID. Copy the Value immediately. Value is basically an API key, This is your actual client secret it goes into the ZADF_CONFIG table in SAP.

Important: Azure shows the Value only once. If you navigate away from this page without copying it, you will not be able to retrieve it again. You would have to delete the secret and create a new one.

Step 5: Configure API Permissions

Go to API Permissions → Add a Permission → Select Microsoft Graph → Select Application permissions (not Delegated) → Search for User.Read.All → Add it.

Note: While Creating a new API permission make sure to go with Application permissions are required because SAP uses the OAuth2 client credentials flow which is a server-to-server call with no interactive user sign-in.

Step 6: Grant Admin Consent

After adding the permission, click "Grant admin consent for <your tenant name>" → Confirm by clicking Yes.

Your tenant name in the sense is the new created tenant in our first Step

Verify that the Status column should show a green checkmark with "Granted for <your tenant name>" next to each permission. If the button is greyed out or shows a different tenant name, you are not an administrator of that tenant.

With this, the Azure side configuration is complete.

 

Part B: SAP Side Configuration

Initial Step: Install the ABAP SDK for Azure

The ABAP SDK provides the classes, interfaces, configuration tables, and demo programs needed to communicate with Azure. Refer to the official installation guide on GitHub: https://github.com/Microsoft/ABAP-SDK-for-Azure

Step 1: Create the RFC Destination for Azure AD

Open transaction SM59 and create a new RFC destination of connection type G (HTTP Connection to External Server).

Step 2: Configure Technical Settings

Now configure Target Host, service Port and Path prefix with the following values:

login.microsoftonline.com, 443, /<YOUR_TENANT_ID>/oauth2/token

Replace <YOUR_TENANT_ID> with the Tenant ID you noted in Step 3 of the Azure configuration. Make sure there is a forward slash at the beginning of the path prefix.

Step 3: Configure Logon & Security

Now navigate to Logon & Security Tab under it configure the following, SSL->Active (select the radio button) and SSL Certificate       DFAULT SSL Client (Standard)

Save the destination. Do not test the connection yet it will fail until the SSL certificates are imported.

Step 4: Download SSL Certificates

SAP needs to trust the SSL certificate from login.microsoftonline.com to establish a secure HTTPS connection. Without this, SAP will throw a SSSLERR_PEER_CERT_UNTRUSTED error.

To download the certificates, open Chrome and navigate to: https://login.microsoftonline.com/<YOUR_TENANT_ID>/oauth2/token

You will see an error page saying "The endpoint only accepts POST, OPTIONS requests." This is normal the token endpoint only accepts POST requests. You are here only to download the certificate.

Click the lock icon next to the URL → View Certificate → Go to the Certification Path tab → Export every certificate in the chain

Warning: You must export ALL certificates in the chain not just one. If you miss the root or intermediate certificate, the SSL handshake will still fail.

Step 5: Import Certificates into STRUST

Contact your Basis team and share the downloaded certificates to STRUST and restart ICM

The "Add to Certificate List" step is critical importing alone loads the certificate but does not add it to the trust store. Without this step, the handshake will still fail.

Step 6: Test the RFC Connection

After the certificates are imported and the ICM is restarted, go back to SM59 and test the connection. You should see HTTP 200 OK. If you still get the SSL error, verify that all certificates in the chain were imported and added to the certificate list.

Step 7: Configure the ABAP SDK Tables

The ABAP SDK uses three configuration tables. Each Azure service interface requires entries

ZREST_CONFIG - Interface to RFC Mapping

This is the master table that links an Interface ID to an RFC destination.

Field

Value

Interface ID

DEMO_AZ_AD

RFC Destination

AZURE_ABAPSDKKDEMO-AAD (your SM59 destination)

ZREST_CONF_MISC - Operational Settings

This table controls the HTTP method, retry logic, and alert settings.

Field

Value

Interface ID

DEMO_AZ_AD

METHOD

POST (the OAuth token endpoint only accepts POST)

MAX_RETRY

1

EMAIL_ID

Your email address (for failure alerts)

MAIL_BODY_TXT

Error text identifier

RETRY_METHOD

Leave blank

ZADF_CONFIG — Azure-Specific Configuration

This table holds the authentication type, client secret, and processing mode.

Field

Value

Interface ID

DEMO_AZ_AD

Interface Type

Azure Active Directory (select from dropdown)

SAS_KEY

Paste your client secret VALUE from Azure Step 4

URI

Leave blank

SERVICE_TYPE

S (Synchronous)

IS_TRY

Leave blank

This table holds the authentication type, client secret, and processing mode.

Important: The Interface ID must be exactly the same across all three tables. It is case-sensitive.

Important: The Interface Type in ZADF_CONFIG determines which ABAP class the SDK factory creates. For AAD token generation, it must be "Azure Active Directory". Setting it to anything else will result in a MOVE_CAST_ERROR at runtime.

With this, the connection between Azure Active Directory and your SAP system is established.

Now let us configure one more RFC destination To fetch data from Azure, we need API access to Microsoft Graph. This requires a separate RFC destination for the Graph API endpoint.

Step 8: Create the RFC Destination for Graph API

Open SM59 and create a new destination:

Step 9: Configure Logon & Security

Follow the same SSL certificate process to download the certificate chain from https://graph.microsoft.com, import into STRUST, restart ICM, and test the connection. You should get HTTP 200.

Step 10: Configure the ABAP SDK Tables for Graph API

now Create entries in all three tables with a new Interface ID:

ZREST_CONFIG

Field

Value

Interface ID

AZ_GRAPH

RFC Destination

AZURE_GRAPH

ZREST_CONF_MISC

Field

Value

Interface ID

AZ_GRAPH

METHOD

GET

MAX_RETRY

1

ZADF_CONFIG

Field

Value

Interface ID

AZ_GRAPH

Interface Type

Microsoft Graph (select from dropdown)

SAS_KEY

Same client secret VALUE as DEMO_AZ_AD

URI

Leave blank

SERVICE_TYPE

S

IS_TRY

Leave blank

Important: The Interface Type for the Graph API interface must be "Microsoft Graph" not "Azure Active Directory". The factory class uses this value to instantiate the correct service class. Wrong type means wrong class, which means a MOVE_CAST_ERROR.

With this, the connection between Microsoft Graph API and your SAP system is established.

Now let us test the connection with The ABAP SDKs existing program this includes a demo program called ZADF_DEMO_AZURE_GRAPH that can fetch users from Azure AD.

Step 11: Open and Update the Program

Go to transaction SE38 and open program ZADF_DEMO_AZURE_GRAPH. Update the Interface ID constants at the top of the program to match your configuration:

Step 12: Execute

Run the program (F8). On the selection screen:

Field

Value

Select

"Read Users" radio button

Azure Client ID

Your Application (Client) ID from Azure Step 3

Step 13: Review the Output

The program will request an AAD token from login.microsoftonline.com, call the Microsoft Graph API /users endpoint with that token, and display the user data in JSON format.

If the program returns user data, your end-to-end integration is working. SAP is authenticating with Azure AD, calling Microsoft Graph API, and receiving data all of this from ABAP.

 

 

What's Next

That's the complete technical setup of Azure side and SAP side. Two RFC destinations, three config tables each, and a working demo program pulling live user data from Azure Active Directory into SAP.

But fetching data through a demo program is just the foundation. In Part 3, I'll show you how to take this further by building a reusable ABAP class that wraps the entire Azure authentication and Graph API flow into a clean interface that can be consumed in to FPM UI in MDG Business Partner screen. That's where this moves from a technical demo to a practical MDG solution.

Stay tuned.

entry.title: New SAP Data Architecture Learning Journey & SAP Certified Data Architect Certification is now Live
entry.content:
TLDR: The Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Learning Journey is now live on learning.sap and alongside it the New Role Play based SAP Certified Data Architect certification is now available in this learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-a-certified-sap-data-architect

Data Architecture Learning Begins here!

Early in my career, I sat in a room full of architects debating a major platform decision. The conversation covered integration patterns, application landscapes, and total cost of ownership. No one discussed the data. Not the quality of it, not how it would flow, not how it would be governed. We treated data as the responsibility of someone else (master data team and BW team) in a different meeting. Years later, we are seeing AI initiatives slow down because of untrustworthy data, I understand exactly what was missing from that room.

Data architecture has spent years as a supporting discipline important, but rarely the headline. That has changed. The shift to agentic AI has moved data architecture from a back-office concern to a front-line competitive capability.

Agentic AI systems do not simply retrieve information. They reason, plan, and act. They require data that is real-time, contextually grounded, semantically rich, and governed with precision. An agent operating on stale, fragmented, or poorly modelled data does not just produce mediocre outputs it can produce incorrect actions. At enterprise scale, that is a governance and business risk problem, not just a technical one.

The architects who will lead in this environment are those who can hold both conversations at once: the strategic (how data drives business value) and the technical (how to design the platforms, integration patterns, and governance structures that make that value real).  

That is exactly what we have tried to address with the New Data Architecture learning Course (Advanced) and a New Data Architect Certification from SAP

Introducing the Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Course

The Mastering SAP Data Architecture Advanced Learning Journey (course BDCDA2) is now live on learning.sap.com. Some of you might have done ‘Becoming a Data Architect – Foundational Course’ which was launched last year and this new course takes a deeper look at the topics related to Data Architecture.

Across four-unit journey designed for architects who are ready to move beyond foundational concepts and into the architecture decisions, framework synthesis, and platform-specific depth that define effective data leadership in the agentic AI era.

  1.  Unit 1: Architecting Data Strategy for the Agentic AI Era

The journey opens with strategy, because that is where most data initiatives fail first. Unit 1 builds the five foundational pillars of data strategy: Governance, Data literacy, Stewardship, Architecture, and Technology enablement and connects each directly to AI readiness. It examines what agentic AI demands from data pipelines that traditional analytics architectures were not built to provide: real-time streaming, semantic enrichment, and contextual grounding. It then moves to leadership and culture, the organizational factors that determine whether a data strategy survives contact with reality and closes with a framework for measuring data strategy success and analyzing real-world value patterns.

  1. Unit 2: Integrating Frameworks for Data Architecture

Architecture without a framework produces inconsistency. Unit 2 examines the three frameworks that define the professional standard in this field: TOGAF, DAMA-DMBOK, and the SAP Enterprise Architecture Framework and, guides how to synthesize them rather than apply them in isolation. It then applies that synthesis to two of the most significant architectural paradigms in the market today: Data Mesh and Data Fabric.

  1.  Unit 3: Harmonizing SAP Business Data Cloud Within the Open Data Ecosystem

Unit 3 is where strategy and frameworks meet the SAP platform. It begins with BDC Connect and the Delta Sharing protocol the mechanism that enables zero-copy data sharing across ecosystem boundaries and then works through each major integration scenario in the open data ecosystem: Databricks, Snowflake (including all three connectivity options), Microsoft Fabric, and Google Big Query. The unit closes with a dedicated lesson on data federation performance optimization the practical trade-offs between federation, replication, and zero-copy sharing that every architect advising on SAP Business Data Cloud deployments needs to understand cold.

  1. Unit 4: Designing Advanced Data Models and Architecture for SAP Business Data Cloud

The final unit addresses SAP Business Data Cloud as a Business Data Fabric how it implements federation, replication, and zero-copy sharing to connect heterogeneous data sources while preserving business context and semantic coherence. It covers Data Mesh in SAP BDC, including how Datasphere Spaces implement the four Data Mesh principles in practice. It then moves to one of the most consequential topics in enterprise AI architecture: semantic models and knowledge graphs as the foundation for trustworthy AI and RAG-based systems.

 Introducing the SAP Certified Data Architect Certification

Alongside the learning journey, we have launched the SAP Certified Data Architect certification.

The SAP Certified Data Architect is an AI role-play-based certification that assesses how well candidates understand modern data architecture in the context of strategic business success. It covers the five major architectural paradigms: Data Warehouse, Data Lake, Data Mesh, Data Fabric, and Data Lakehouse, alongside the core elements of data maturity: governance frameworks, data security, and business-aligned data strategy. It uses interactive question-and-answer format, assessing the understanding of SAP Data Architecture in the agentic AI era specifically the ability to synthesize and articulate how SAP Business Data Cloud enables governed, semantically coherent data access across hybrid cloud environments.

Note: It is recommended that the candidate completes the 1) Becoming an SAP Data Architect and 2) Mastering SAP Data Architecture courses before attempting the certificate to ensure successful completion.

 Who This Is For

The learning journey and certification are designed for practitioners who are already working in the data and architecture space and want to develop the rigor, depth, and formal recognition to operate at senior level. That includes data architects designing enterprise data platforms, enterprise architects expanding their data architecture competency, analytics and BI leaders responsible for governed data access, and technical decision-makers advising customers on SAP Business Data Cloud strategy.

 If you are starting your data architecture journey, the foundational 'Becoming a Data Architect' learning journey on learning.sap.com is the right starting point. The advanced journey picks up where that one ends.

Getting Started

  1. The Becoming a Certified SAP Data Architect Learning Journey live now for both internal and external learners: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-a-certified-sap-data-architect
  2. SAP Certified Data Architect certification registration: https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-data-architect-sap-business-data-cloud

A Final Thought

In the agentic AI era, the constraint on enterprise intelligence is rarely the algorithm. It is the architecture underneath it the decisions made about how data is structured, governed, connected, and made meaningful. Those decisions require a specific set of skills. This learning journey and certification exist to build them.

The practitioners who invest in that depth now will be the ones designing the data foundations that AI-enabled enterprises run on. That work is not infrastructure. It is strategy.

Happy Learning!

P.S: A Big Thank you to @JaSoN_Luo , Sabrina Brück and @Basabdatta_Sengupta  for making this course and certification Happen!

SAP Business Data Cloud
